# UX Checklist: Order Policy Confirmation

**Purpose**: Validate that the UX requirements for the order policy confirmation flow are complete, clear, measurable, and ready for implementation.
**Created**: 2026-03-25
**Feature**: /Users/subho/sources/repos/friendly-octo-giggle/specs/003-order-policy-dialog/spec.md

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the dialog's required content sections fully specified, including itemized line entries, subtotal, shipping, total, policy copy, support email, and acknowledgment control? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Spec FR-002a, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the Help page and Returns page UX update requirements explicit enough to define what content must match checkout and what layout freedom remains page-specific? [Completeness, Spec FR-014, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the spec define whether the dialog must show customization notes, variation labels, or other per-item details beyond the pricing breakdown? [Gap, Spec FR-002, Spec FR-002a]
- [ ] CHK004 Are the dismissal paths fully documented for all intended UI affordances, including cancel action, close action, and backdrop/outside-click behavior if supported? [Completeness, Spec FR-012, Spec Edge Cases]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is "explicitly confirm" sufficiently clarified in the requirements so there is no ambiguity between opening the dialog, checking the acknowledgment box, and activating `Confirm and Place Order`? [Clarity, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the phrase "full cart pricing breakdown" specific enough to avoid multiple interpretations of what must be shown in the dialog? [Clarity, Spec FR-002a]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the required policy wording precise enough to prevent softer UX copy that could weaken the no-refund, post-shipment no-cancellation, or damaged-only return meaning? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-004, Spec FR-005, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-007]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the acknowledgment label itself defined or constrained enough to ensure the shopper is clearly acknowledging the policy terms rather than a generic confirmation? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-011a]

## Consistency & Measurability

- [ ] CHK009 Are the checkout dialog, Help page, and Returns page requirements consistent about the same support email, replacement-only remedy, and customer-paid return shipping responsibility? [Consistency, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-009, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-014]
- [ ] CHK010 Do the user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria all align on whether the key UX outcome is policy review before submission rather than simply adding another confirmation click? [Consistency, Spec User Story 1, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-011, Spec SC-001]
- [ ] CHK011 Can the UX success criteria be objectively verified from the written requirements, especially the under-60-second completion target and user comprehension targets? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002, Spec SC-003]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK012 Are requirements defined for the zero-progression path where the shopper opens the dialog, reads the policy, and decides not to place the order? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1, Spec FR-012, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK013 Are multi-item and dense-cart scenarios covered well enough to establish how the dialog should preserve readability when many items and totals are shown together with policy text? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-002a]
- [ ] CHK014 Does the spec define the UX expectation when policy content cannot be displayed, beyond blocking submission, such as whether the shopper sees an explanatory message or recovery guidance? [Gap, Spec Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional UX Requirements

- [ ] CHK015 Are accessibility requirements explicit for keyboard navigation, focus handling, dialog labeling, and checkbox interaction in the confirmation flow? [Gap, Non-Functional, Spec FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK016 Are mobile and small-viewport readability requirements defined for fitting the pricing summary, policy sections, and acknowledgment control into one review experience? [Gap, Non-Functional]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
